name: Liechtenstein

visa_free.nationality: All Schengen Area member state nationals
visa_free.notes: Liechtenstein is a Schengen member. Nationals of all other Schengen member states (Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Croatia, Cyprus, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Ireland, Italy, Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Monaco, Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Romania, San Marino, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden) face no border controls and have freedom of movement under EEA/Schengen rules. Liechtenstein does not issue its own visas; Swiss Schengen rules apply identically.

No visa required for stays up to 90 days or more than 90 days.

visa_policy.notes: Liechtenstein does not issue its own visas. It is a full Schengen member and applies identical visa rules to Switzerland. The official Liechtenstein Embassy in the USA states: 'without exception the same visa requirements apply to travelers to Liechtenstein as they do to travelers to Switzerland.' There are no border controls between Liechtenstein and Switzerland. The visa list is sourced from Switzerland SEM Annex CH-1 List 1 (version 3 April 2026). 90-day stays are calculated within any 180-day period (Schengen rules). V1 condition means: no visa required for tourism/business up to 90 days, but visa required if working in certain sectors or if working more than 8 days/year in any sector.

conditional_access.conditions: Diplomatic passport only. Bilateral visa exemption agreement signed 19 March 2004, entered into force 1 July 2004. Non-working stays of a maximum of 90 days within any 180-day period. Liechtenstein applies identical Swiss bilateral agreements as confirmed by liechtensteinusa.org.

Official passports only. Bilateral visa exemption agreement signed 15 January / 28 May 1991, entered into force 28 May 1991. Non-working stays of a maximum of 90 days within any 180-day period. SEM source lists only 'Official' passport type for Algeria — diplomatic not covered.

cbi.notes: Liechtenstein has no citizenship-by-investment (CBI) program. The standard naturalization path requires 30 years of continuous residence, or ordinary naturalization after 10 years of residence subject to approval by a public ballot of the local municipality (in practice extremely rarely granted). Liechtenstein does not permit dual nationality. Sources: www.llv.li (Migration and Passport Office) and www.liechtensteinusa.org.

programs.name: Residence Permit (B) Draw for EEA Nationals — With Gainful Employment
programs.notes: EEA nationals only (Swiss nationals excluded). Annual quota of 56 permits for gainful employment, allocated 50% by lottery (twice yearly, spring and autumn) and 50% by government decision. B permit is issued for more than 12 months and is renewable. After 5 years of lawful residence, permanent residence (C permit) may be obtained. Citizenship requires 30 years continuous residence (or ordinary naturalization after 10 years subject to municipal ballot). No monetary investment requirement — residence is earned through employment. Non-EEA/non-Swiss nationals may not participate in the draw; they must apply for direct-issue permits in exceptional cases of national interest only.

programs.name: Residence Permit (B) Draw for EEA Nationals — Without Gainful Employment
programs.notes: EEA nationals only (Swiss and UK nationals excluded as of Brexit). Annual quota of 16 permits for non-gainful residence, allocated by lottery (twice yearly). Applicants must demonstrate sufficient financial means to sustain themselves without recourse to social assistance. B permit annual and renewable. After 5 years, permanent residence (C permit) may be obtained. Citizenship requires 30 years continuous residence. No minimum monetary investment amount specified in official sources.

programs.name: Short-term Residence Permit (L) for Qualified Specialists — Non-EEA Nationals
programs.eligibility: Non-EEA and non-Swiss nationals who are managers, specialists, or other qualified employees who have completed an apprenticeship or have many years of professional experience. Must be sponsored by a Liechtenstein company.
programs.notes: L permit allows residence for a maximum of 12 months, extendable once for up to 6 months upon proof of extraordinary need. Application is made by the Liechtenstein employer through the Migration and Passport Office (APA). No dedicated digital nomad, startup, or fast-track talent visa found on official llv.li sources.

agreements: EEA (European Economic Area) freedom of movement

validation_notes: Re-validation performed 2026-06-14 against live official sources.

CORRECTED: Algeria conditional_access entry had passport_types ['diplomatic','official'] — corrected to ['official'] only. Live SEM bilateral agreements page (sem.admin.ch) lists Algeria's agreement as covering only 'Official' passports, consistent with the agreement entered into force 28 May 1991.

CORRECTED: Turkey conditional_access conditions text said 'Diplomatic and other official passports' — corrected to 'Diplomatic passports only'. SEM source confirms only diplomatic passport type for Turkey in the bilateral agreement.
CORRECTED: RBI non-gainful B permit lottery official_url had a path typo ('national-administrative' instead of 'national-administration') — fixed to match the confirmed llv.li URL structure.

UPDATED: ETIAS ETA entry notes updated to reflect Q4 2026 planned mandatory date with 6-month transitional period (confirmed via ETIAS search results and ec.europa.eu; llv.li ETIAS page returns HTTP 403).
VERIFIED UNCHANGED: All 52 bilateral diplomatic visa exemption agreements confirmed against live SEM page. All entries verified correct for country name, passport types, and dates.
VERIFIED UNCHANGED: liechtensteinusa.org confirms identical Swiss/Schengen visa rules apply to Liechtenstein.

VERIFIED UNCHANGED: CBI program correctly listed as not existing. Naturalization requires 30 years ordinary residence (with years from birth to 20 counted twice) or 10-year residence subject to municipal ballot vote.
VERIFIED UNCHANGED: RBI B permit lottery (EEA only) for employment (56 permits/year) and non-employment (16 permits/year) confirmed as still operational per llv.li search results.
gaps: Exact financial sufficiency thresholds for the non-gainful employment residence permit draw are not published verbatim in any accessible official English-language page (llv.li returns HTTP 403 on direct page fetch).
Exact processing times for the Short-term Residence Permit (L) not found on official sources.
ETIAS exact mandatory implementation date: Q4 2026 is planned per search results but not confirmed on official llv.li page (page was 403 blocked during this validation).
No official English-language page enumerating all specific permit fees was accessible.

Conditional_access type A (credential-based): No official Swiss/Liechtenstein source was found granting visa-free entry based on holding a non-Schengen foreign visa (US, UK, Canada, Japan, etc.). Aggregator sites claim such rules exist but no official sem.admin.ch or llv.li page confirms them. NOT recorded per hard rules.

Conditional_access type B (OCI/special status): No official Swiss/Liechtenstein source found granting any special entry privilege to Indian OCI cardholders or similar diaspora status holders. NOT recorded per hard rules.

The full alphabetical country list for each diplomatic passport sub-category (M:D, M:D,S, M:D,S,SP, M:D,S,SP,OP etc.) on sem.admin.ch was not accessible as individual pages returned only framework text without specific country lists; all diplomatic passport entries are drawn from the dedicated bilateral agreements page (sem.admin.ch/...visumbefreiung-dipl.html) which is the primary official registry.

Schengen Area residence permit holders (any nationality) may travel within Schengen including Liechtenstein using their permit, but this is an EU/Schengen-wide rule (Article 21 CISA), not a Liechtenstein-specific conditional entry rule, and is therefore not recorded as a Liechtenstein conditional_access entry.
llv.li returns HTTP 403 Forbidden on all direct page fetches, preventing full verification of permit quota numbers and financial requirements directly from the source.

outbound_official.note: No official Liechtenstein government outbound visa-list page located. The Liechtenstein National Administration (llv.li) does not publish a country-by-country list of destinations where Liechtenstein ordinary-passport holders may travel without a prior consular visa. The llv.li 'Mobilität, Reisen & Ausland' section directs citizens to the Swiss Federal Department of Foreign Affairs (eda.admin.ch) for travel advice, as Liechtenstein relies on Swiss diplomatic missions abroad. The eda.admin.ch domain also does not publish a standalone outbound visa-free list for Swiss/Liechtenstein citizens in the manner of India's mea.gov.in/vffin. Rely on destination-side data.

sources_checked.description: Official Embassy of Liechtenstein in the USA — visa information page confirming identical Schengen/Swiss visa rules apply to Liechtenstein

Swiss State Secretariat for Migration (SEM) — Annex CH-1 List 1: Overview of ID and visa provisions according to nationality, version 3 April 2026. This list applies to Liechtenstein as confirmed by official Liechtenstein sources.
Swiss SEM — Bilateral visa exemption agreements for diplomatic passport holders (46 agreements as of June 2026). These apply to Liechtenstein as it uses identical Swiss Schengen rules.
Swiss SEM — List of visa requirements for holders of diplomatic, service, special, or official passports (index page, version 3 April 2026).

Swiss SEM — Entry with visa page: confirms that entry requires valid C visa (Schengen), D visa (national), or Schengen residence permit; no credential-based (non-Schengen visa holder) exemption found.
Liechtenstein National Administration — Migration and Passport Office main page (HTTP 403 on direct fetch, but confirmed via search results)
Official Liechtenstein ETIAS information page (HTTP 403 on direct fetch, confirmed via search results)
Official llv.li page on B permit lottery for gainful employment (HTTP 403 on direct fetch, confirmed via search results)
Official llv.li page on B permit lottery for non-gainful employment (HTTP 403 on direct fetch, confirmed via search results)
